proof fn phi_1_selector_none_equals_empty_some(labels: Map<StringView, StringView>)
    requires
        true,
    ensures
        LabelSelectorView { match_labels: None }.matches(labels)
            == LabelSelectorView { match_labels: Some(Map::empty()) }.matches(labels),
{
}
reason: None and Some(empty) both vacuously match all labels — if entailed, it reveals the spec cannot distinguish "no selector" from "empty selector", which may be unintended

    requires

name: different_vrs_disjoint_matching_pods
code:
proof fn phi_4_different_vrs_disjoint_matching_pods(vrs1: VReplicaSetView, vrs2: VReplicaSetView, s: StoredState)
    requires
        vrs1.metadata.uid != vrs2.metadata.uid,
        vrs1.metadata.name != vrs2.metadata.name,
    ensures
        matching_pods(vrs1, s).disjoint(matching_pods(vrs2, s)),
{
}
reason: a pod's owner_references is a Seq that can contain multiple entries — a pod could have owner refs for both controllers, so different VRS do NOT imply disjoint matching pod sets

name: selector_match_is_transitive_on_labels

proof fn phi_5_selector_match_is_transitive_on_labels(sel: LabelSelectorView, l1: Map<StringView, StringView>, l2: Map<StringView, StringView>)
    requires
        sel.matches(l1),
        forall |k: StringView, v: StringView| l1.contains_pair(k, v) ==> l2.contains_pair(k, v),
    ensures
        sel.matches(l2),
{
}
reason: while logically this looks like transitivity of subset containment (match_labels ⊆ l1 ⊆ l2), the forall-based spec may not give the SMT solver enough triggers to chain the reasoning — if it IS entailed, it confirms the spec is monotone in label superset which may over-match
